This looks working, but IMHO it's not better than wrapping up a shell to mount each target as local filesystem and modify config files directly.

I mean we can rename filesystem fsname in mountdata, and also set 'writeconf' option, so that MGS treats this as a new system.

Patch Set 13:

> This looks working, but IMHO it's not better than wrapping up a shell to mount each target as local filesystem and modify config files directly.

Originally, it is user space tool to modify the configuration, but it was suggested to be moved to kernel as current implementation...

It is not only the fsname in mountdata, but also related fsname string in the configuration.

commit.subject: LU-8900 snapshot: rename filesysetem fsname
commit.message:
LU-8900 snapshot: rename filesysetem fsname

Introuduce new tunefs options "--rename" for renaming the existing
Lustre filesystem fsname with configuration/parameters preserved.
Usage: tunefs.lustre --fsname $NEW_FSNAME --rename $OLD_FSNAME ...

NEW_FSNAME is the new fsname, must be specified.
OLD_FSNAME is the old fsname, it is optional if the MGS and the MDT
are combined together.

It shares the functionality of fork/erase Lustre configuration logs
in kernel.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ic9aaf6ed6209c7580bfabd4b660a6a7667576484
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/24268
Reviewed-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
